{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Fleet Manager",
  "description": "CW Real Estate is a forward-thinking real estate firm redefining the property experience across Nigeria and beyond. We specialize in premium residential and commercial properties, offering tailored solutions combining innovation, expertise, and an in-depth market understanding.

Driven by a passion for excellence, we provide end-to-end services&mdash;from property acquisition and development to sales, leasing, and property management. Our experienced professionals are committed to delivering value, integrity, and results for every client.

At CW Real Estate, we don&rsquo;t just build and sell properties&mdash;we create communities, investments, and lifestyle opportunities that stand the test of time. Whether you&rsquo;re a first-time buyer, seasoned investor, or corporate partner, we&rsquo;re here to help you make smart, informed real estate decisions.

We are&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Fleet Manager

Job Location: Ikoyi, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking an experienced and highly organized Fleet Manager to oversee the management, maintenance, repairs of vehicle(fix vehicles himself)and operational efficiency of the company&rsquo;s fleet vehicles.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for ensuring all vehicles are properly maintained, compliant with safety regulations, and operating efficiently to support business operations.
	The successful candidate must possess strong leadership, technical, and administrative skills, with prior experience managing corporate fleets and a solid background in vehicle maintenance and logistics coordination.


Key Responsibilities


	Oversee the day-to-day operations of the company&rsquo;s fleet.
	Monitor vehicle usage, maintenance schedules, servicing, and repairs.
	Ensure all vehicles are in good working condition and comply with safety and regulatory standards.
	Maintain accurate records of vehicle servicing, fueling, insurance, licenses, and inspections.
	Coordinate with drivers, mechanics.
	Monitor fuel consumption and implement cost-control measures.
	Investigate and report vehicle incidents, accidents, or damages.
	Develop preventive maintenance programs to minimize vehicle downtime.
	Track fleet performance and prepare operational reports for management.
	Ensure proper allocation and utilization of company vehicles.
	Supervise and evaluate drivers and fleet support personnel where necessary.
	Recommend fleet replacement and upgrade plans when required.


Requirements and Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Mechanical Engineering or related field.
	3&ndash;5 years of proven experience as a Fleet Manager or in a similar role.
	Strong knowledge of vehicle maintenance, fleet operations, and transportation management.
	Excellent organizational and problem-solving skills.
	Ability to manage multiple tasks and work under pressure.
	Strong leadership and communication skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and fleet management systems/software.
	Knowledge of safety regulations and vehicle compliance requirements.
	Valid driver&rsquo;s license is an added advantage.


Key Competencies:


	Technical and mechanical knowledge.
	Attention to detail.
	Time management.
	Analytical thinking.
	Cost management and budgeting.
	Effective communication.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: careers@cwlagos.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Fleet Manager Job at CW Real Estate




        
    

        
        CW Real Estate is a forward-thinking real estate firm redefining the property experience across Nigeria and beyond. We specialize in premium residential and commercial properties, offering tailored solutions combining innovation, expertise, and an in-depth market understanding.

Driven by a passion for excellence, we provide end-to-end services—from property acquisition and development to sales, leasing, and property management. Our experienced professionals are committed to delivering value, integrity, and results for every client.
     

            
    



At CW Real Estate, we don’t just build and sell properties—we create communities, investments, and lifestyle opportunities that stand the test of time. Whether you’re a first-time buyer, seasoned investor, or corporate partner, we’re here to help you make smart, informed real estate decisions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Fleet Manager

Job Location: Ikoyi, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking an experienced and highly organized Fleet Manager to oversee the management, maintenance, repairs of vehicle(fix vehicles himself)and operational efficiency of the company’s fleet vehicles.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for ensuring all vehicles are properly maintained, compliant with safety regulations, and operating efficiently to support business operations.
	The successful candidate must possess strong leadership, technical, and administrative skills, with prior experience managing corporate fleets and a solid background in vehicle maintenance and logistics coordination.


Key Responsibilities


	Oversee the day-to-day operations of the company’s fleet.
	Monitor vehicle usage, maintenance schedules, servicing, and repairs.
	Ensure all vehicles are in good working condition and comply with safety and regulatory standards.
	Maintain accurate records of vehicle servicing, fueling, insurance, licenses, and inspections.
	Coordinate with drivers, mechanics.
	Monitor fuel consumption and implement cost-control measures.
	Investigate and report vehicle incidents, accidents, or damages.
	Develop preventive maintenance programs to minimize vehicle downtime.
	Track fleet performance and prepare operational reports for management.
	Ensure proper allocation and utilization of company vehicles.
	Supervise and evaluate drivers and fleet support personnel where necessary.
	Recommend fleet replacement and upgrade plans when required.


Requirements and Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in Mechanical Engineering or related field.
	3–5 years of proven experience as a Fleet Manager or in a similar role.
	Strong knowledge of vehicle maintenance, fleet operations, and transportation management.
	Excellent organizational and problem-solving skills.
	Ability to manage multiple tasks and work under pressure.
	Strong leadership and communication skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and fleet management systems/software.
	Knowledge of safety regulations and vehicle compliance requirements.
	Valid driver’s license is an added advantage.


Key Competencies:


	Technical and mechanical knowledge.
	Attention to detail.
	Time management.
	Analytical thinking.
	Cost management and budgeting.
	Effective communication.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Operations Supervisor",
  "description": "JEV Consulting - Our client, a major player in the logistics industry is&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Operations Supervisor

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Work Days &amp; Time: 4PM &ndash; 7AM (Monday &ndash; Saturday)

Job Purpose


	Our client is requires the services of Operations Supervisor for immediate employment. Proximity to IKEJA GRA will be an advantage. Candidates living at Ikeja, Mafoluku, Oshodi and its environs will be considered.
	The Operations Supervisor is responsible for supervising and coordinating all warehouse operations during assigned shifts to ensure the efficient receiving, sorting, scanning, loading, dispatch, and movement of parcels. The role ensures that operational targets, customer delivery timelines, and Company standards are consistently achieved while maintaining discipline, safety, and productivity within the warehouse.
	The Operations Supervisor is responsible for leading warehouse teams, monitoring operational performance, resolving operational challenges, and ensuring strict compliance with Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs).


Key Responsibilities
Warehouse Operations Management:


	Supervise all warehouse operations during assigned shifts.
	Ensure all inbound and outbound parcels are received, sorted, scanned, and dispatched accurately.
	Monitor warehouse workflow to achieve operational efficiency.
	Ensure all parcels are processed within approved timelines.
	Minimize parcel loss, damage, and misrouting.
	Ensure warehouse activities comply with Company policies and SOPs.


Staff Supervision &amp; Leadership:


	Supervise Team Leads, Sorters, Warehouse Assistants, and other operational staff.
	Allocate daily responsibilities based on operational requirements.
	Conduct pre-shift and post-shift briefings.
	Monitor employee productivity and performance.
	Coach, mentor, and motivate team members to achieve operational targets.
	Enforce discipline and maintain a professional work environment.
	Recommend staff training and development where necessary.


Attendance &amp; Manpower Management:


	Monitor staff attendance and punctuality.
	Ensure employees resume work on time and remain at their assigned workstations throughout their shift.
	Prepare daily attendance reports.
	Report absenteeism, lateness, misconduct, or unauthorized movement to the Human Resources Department.
	Ensure adequate manpower is available for all operational activities.
	Recommend additional manpower where operational demands require.


Parcel Sorting &amp; Dispatch:


	Ensure parcels are sorted accurately according to destination, delivery route, and hub.
	Monitor scanning activities to ensure accurate system updates.
	Verify parcel quantities before loading and dispatch.
	Ensure priority shipments are processed promptly.
	Prevent dispatch errors and parcel misrouting.
	Monitor dispatch completion to meet delivery timelines.


Operational Performance Management:


	Monitor daily operational targets and Key Performance Indicators (KPIs).
	Ensure all customer delivery timelines, including priority client timelines (e.g., TEMU), are achieved.
	Identify operational bottlenecks and implement corrective actions.
	Reduce warehouse backlog through effective planning and supervision.
	Continuously improve warehouse productivity and efficiency.


Quality Control &amp; Compliance:


	Ensure compliance with all Company Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs).
	Monitor parcel handling standards to reduce damage and losses.
	Ensure accurate documentation of all operational activities.
	Support Quality Control investigations relating to missing, damaged, or delayed parcels.
	Ensure compliance with operational audits and inspections.


Health, Safety &amp; Security:


	Maintain a safe, clean, and organized warehouse environment.
	Ensure employees comply with health and safety regulations.
	Report workplace accidents, hazards, and security incidents immediately.
	Ensure Company property and operational equipment are safeguarded.
	Enforce security procedures relating to parcel handling and warehouse access.


Equipment &amp; Asset Management:


	Monitor the proper use of warehouse equipment, scanners, laptops, printers, pallet trucks, and other operational tools.
	Report damaged equipment promptly.
	Ensure equipment is maintained and properly stored after use.
	Support periodic inventory and asset verification exercises.


Reporting:


	Prepare and submit accurate operational reports, including:
	Daily Shift Reports
	Attendance Reports
	Parcel Sorting Reports
	Dispatch Reports
	Warehouse Backlog Reports
	Productivity Reports
	Incident Reports
	Equipment Damage Reports
	Operational Improvement Reports


Customer Service Support:


	Work closely with the Customer Service and Quality Control teams to resolve customer complaints relating to warehouse operations.
	Investigate delayed shipments, misplaced parcels, and operational discrepancies.
	Provide timely feedback on operational issues affecting customer satisfaction.


Continuous Improvement:


	Identify opportunities to improve warehouse processes.
	Recommend productivity improvement initiatives.
	Participate in process improvement projects.
	Implement Management directives aimed at improving operational efficiency.
	Encourage teamwork and continuous learning within the operations team.
	Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)
	Parcel sorting accuracy.
	Dispatch completion within approved timelines.
	Warehouse productivity.
	Reduction in parcel loss and damage.
	Compliance with TEMU and other customer delivery timelines.
	Staff attendance and punctuality.
	Reduction in operational errors.
	Warehouse turnaround time.
	Employee productivity.
	Compliance with SOPs.
	Safety compliance.
	Customer complaint reduction.
	Warehouse cleanliness and organization.


Remuneration
N200,000 &ndash; N250,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: recruitment@jevconsulting.com.ng and copy: jobs@jevconsulting.com.ng using the&nbsp;&nbsp;Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Operations Supervisor Job at JEV Consulting




        
    

        
        JEV Consulting - Our client, a major player in the logistics industry is recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Operations Supervisor

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Work Days & Time: 4PM – 7AM (Monday – Saturday)

Job Purpose
     

            
    




	Our client is requires the services of Operations Supervisor for immediate employment. Proximity to IKEJA GRA will be an advantage. Candidates living at Ikeja, Mafoluku, Oshodi and its environs will be considered.
	The Operations Supervisor is responsible for supervising and coordinating all warehouse operations during assigned shifts to ensure the efficient receiving, sorting, scanning, loading, dispatch, and movement of parcels. The role ensures that operational targets, customer delivery timelines, and Company standards are consistently achieved while maintaining discipline, safety, and productivity within the warehouse.
	The Operations Supervisor is responsible for leading warehouse teams, monitoring operational performance, resolving operational challenges, and ensuring strict compliance with Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs).


Key Responsibilities
Warehouse Operations Management:


	Supervise all warehouse operations during assigned shifts.
	Ensure all inbound and outbound parcels are received, sorted, scanned, and dispatched accurately.
	Monitor warehouse workflow to achieve operational efficiency.
	Ensure all parcels are processed within approved timelines.
	Minimize parcel loss, damage, and misrouting.
	Ensure warehouse activities comply with Company policies and SOPs.


Staff Supervision & Leadership:


	Supervise Team Leads, Sorters, Warehouse Assistants, and other operational staff.
	Allocate daily responsibilities based on operational requirements.
	Conduct pre-shift and post-shift briefings.
	Monitor employee productivity and performance.
	Coach, mentor, and motivate team members to achieve operational targets.
	Enforce discipline and maintain a professional work environment.
	Recommend staff training and development where necessary.


Attendance & Manpower Management:


	Monitor staff attendance and punctuality.
	Ensure employees resume work on time and remain at their assigned workstations throughout their shift.
	Prepare daily attendance reports.
	Report absenteeism, lateness, misconduct, or unauthorized movement to the Human Resources Department.
	Ensure adequate manpower is available for all operational activities.
	Recommend additional manpower where operational demands require.


Parcel Sorting & Dispatch:


	Ensure parcels are sorted accurately according to destination, delivery route, and hub.
	Monitor scanning activities to ensure accurate system updates.
	Verify parcel quantities before loading and dispatch.
	Ensure priority shipments are processed promptly.
	Prevent dispatch errors and parcel misrouting.
	Monitor dispatch completion to meet delivery timelines.


Operational Performance Management:


	Monitor daily operational targets and Key Performance Indicators (KPIs).
	Ensure all customer delivery timelines, including priority client timelines (e.g., TEMU), are achieved.
	Identify operational bottlenecks and implement corrective actions.
	Reduce warehouse backlog through effective planning and supervision.
	Continuously improve warehouse productivity and efficiency.


Quality Control & Compliance:


	Ensure compliance with all Company Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs).
	Monitor parcel handling standards to reduce damage and losses.
	Ensure accurate documentation of all operational activities.
	Support Quality Control investigations relating to missing, damaged, or delayed parcels.
	Ensure compliance with operational audits and inspections.


Health, Safety & Security:


	Maintain a safe, clean, and organized warehouse environment.
	Ensure employees comply with health and safety regulations.
	Report workplace accidents, hazards, and security incidents immediately.
	Ensure Company property and operational equipment are safeguarded.
	Enforce security procedures relating to parcel handling and warehouse access.


Equipment & Asset Management:


	Monitor the proper use of warehouse equipment, scanners, laptops, printers, pallet trucks, and other operational tools.
	Report damaged equipment promptly.
	Ensure equipment is maintained and properly stored after use.
	Support periodic inventory and asset verification exercises.


Reporting:


	Prepare and submit accurate operational reports, including:
	Daily Shift Reports
	Attendance Reports
	Parcel Sorting Reports
	Dispatch Reports
	Warehouse Backlog Reports
	Productivity Reports
	Incident Reports
	Equipment Damage Reports
	Operational Improvement Reports


Customer Service Support:


	Work closely with the Customer Service and Quality Control teams to resolve customer complaints relating to warehouse operations.
	Investigate delayed shipments, misplaced parcels, and operational discrepancies.
	Provide timely feedback on operational issues affecting customer satisfaction.


Continuous Improvement:


	Identify opportunities to improve warehouse processes.
	Recommend productivity improvement initiatives.
	Participate in process improvement projects.
	Implement Management directives aimed at improving operational efficiency.
	Encourage teamwork and continuous learning within the operations team.
	Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)
	Parcel sorting accuracy.
	Dispatch completion within approved timelines.
	Warehouse productivity.
	Reduction in parcel loss and damage.
	Compliance with TEMU and other customer delivery timelines.
	Staff attendance and punctuality.
	Reduction in operational errors.
	Warehouse turnaround time.
	Employee productivity.
	Compliance with SOPs.
	Safety compliance.
	Customer complaint reduction.
	Warehouse cleanliness and organization.


Remuneration
N200,000 – N250,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected]g and copy: [email protected]g using the  Job Position as the subject of the mail.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Operations Supervisor",
  "description": "JEV Consulting - Our client, a major player in the logistics industry is&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Operations Supervisor

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
&nbsp;Work Days &amp; Time: 4PM &ndash; 7AM (Monday &ndash; Saturday)

Job Purpose


	Our client is requires the services of Operations Supervisor for immediate employment. Proximity to IKEJA GRA will be an advantage. Candidates living at Ikeja, Mafoluku, Oshodi and its environs will be considered.
	The Operations Supervisor is responsible for supervising and coordinating all warehouse operations during assigned shifts to ensure the efficient receiving, sorting, scanning, loading, dispatch, and movement of parcels. The role ensures that operational targets, customer delivery timelines, and Company standards are consistently achieved while maintaining discipline, safety, and productivity within the warehouse.
	The Operations Supervisor is responsible for leading warehouse teams, monitoring operational performance, resolving operational challenges, and ensuring strict compliance with Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs).


Key Responsibilities
Warehouse Operations Management:


	Supervise all warehouse operations during assigned shifts.
	Ensure all inbound and outbound parcels are received, sorted, scanned, and dispatched accurately.
	Monitor warehouse workflow to achieve operational efficiency.
	Ensure all parcels are processed within approved timelines.
	Minimize parcel loss, damage, and misrouting.
	Ensure warehouse activities comply with Company policies and SOPs.


Staff Supervision &amp; Leadership:


	Supervise Team Leads, Sorters, Warehouse Assistants, and other operational staff.
	Allocate daily responsibilities based on operational requirements.
	Conduct pre-shift and post-shift briefings.
	Monitor employee productivity and performance.
	Coach, mentor, and motivate team members to achieve operational targets.
	Enforce discipline and maintain a professional work environment.
	Recommend staff training and development where necessary.


Attendance &amp; Manpower Management:


	Monitor staff attendance and punctuality.
	Ensure employees resume work on time and remain at their assigned workstations throughout their shift.
	Prepare daily attendance reports.
	Report absenteeism, lateness, misconduct, or unauthorized movement to the Human Resources Department.
	Ensure adequate manpower is available for all operational activities.
	Recommend additional manpower where operational demands require.


Parcel Sorting &amp; Dispatch:


	Ensure parcels are sorted accurately according to destination, delivery route, and hub.
	Monitor scanning activities to ensure accurate system updates.
	Verify parcel quantities before loading and dispatch.
	Ensure priority shipments are processed promptly.
	Prevent dispatch errors and parcel misrouting.
	Monitor dispatch completion to meet delivery timelines.


Operational Performance Management:


	Monitor daily operational targets and Key Performance Indicators (KPIs).
	Ensure all customer delivery timelines, including priority client timelines (e.g., TEMU), are achieved.
	Identify operational bottlenecks and implement corrective actions.
	Reduce warehouse backlog through effective planning and supervision.
	Continuously improve warehouse productivity and efficiency.


Quality Control &amp; Compliance:


	Ensure compliance with all Company Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs).
	Monitor parcel handling standards to reduce damage and losses.
	Ensure accurate documentation of all operational activities.
	Support Quality Control investigations relating to missing, damaged, or delayed parcels.
	Ensure compliance with operational audits and inspections.


Health, Safety &amp; Security:


	Maintain a safe, clean, and organized warehouse environment.
	Ensure employees comply with health and safety regulations.
	Report workplace accidents, hazards, and security incidents immediately.
	Ensure Company property and operational equipment are safeguarded.
	Enforce security procedures relating to parcel handling and warehouse access.


Equipment &amp; Asset Management:


	Monitor the proper use of warehouse equipment, scanners, laptops, printers, pallet trucks, and other operational tools.
	Report damaged equipment promptly.
	Ensure equipment is maintained and properly stored after use.
	Support periodic inventory and asset verification exercises.


Reporting:


	Prepare and submit accurate operational reports, including:
	Daily Shift Reports
	Attendance Reports
	Parcel Sorting Reports
	Dispatch Reports
	Warehouse Backlog Reports
	Productivity Reports
	Incident Reports
	Equipment Damage Reports
	Operational Improvement Reports


Customer Service Support:


	Work closely with the Customer Service and Quality Control teams to resolve customer complaints relating to warehouse operations.
	Investigate delayed shipments, misplaced parcels, and operational discrepancies.
	Provide timely feedback on operational issues affecting customer satisfaction.


Continuous Improvement:


	Identify opportunities to improve warehouse processes.
	Recommend productivity improvement initiatives.
	Participate in process improvement projects.
	Implement Management directives aimed at improving operational efficiency.
	Encourage teamwork and continuous learning within the operations team.
	Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)
	Parcel sorting accuracy.
	Dispatch completion within approved timelines.
	Warehouse productivity.
	Reduction in parcel loss and damage.
	Compliance with TEMU and other customer delivery timelines.
	Staff attendance and punctuality.
	Reduction in operational errors.
	Warehouse turnaround time.
	Employee productivity.
	Compliance with SOPs.
	Safety compliance.
	Customer complaint reduction.
	Warehouse cleanliness and organization.


Remuneration
N200,000 &ndash; N250,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: recruitment@jevconsulting.com.ng and copy: jobs@jevconsulting.com.ng using the&nbsp;&nbsp;Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Jev Consulting"
    
  },

Operations Supervisor Job at JEV Consulting




        
    

        
        JEV Consulting - Our client, a major player in the logistics industry is recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Operations Supervisor

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
 Work Days & Time: 4PM – 7AM (Monday – Saturday)

Job Purpose
     

            
    




	Our client is requires the services of Operations Supervisor for immediate employment. Proximity to IKEJA GRA will be an advantage. Candidates living at Ikeja, Mafoluku, Oshodi and its environs will be considered.
	The Operations Supervisor is responsible for supervising and coordinating all warehouse operations during assigned shifts to ensure the efficient receiving, sorting, scanning, loading, dispatch, and movement of parcels. The role ensures that operational targets, customer delivery timelines, and Company standards are consistently achieved while maintaining discipline, safety, and productivity within the warehouse.
	The Operations Supervisor is responsible for leading warehouse teams, monitoring operational performance, resolving operational challenges, and ensuring strict compliance with Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs).


Key Responsibilities
Warehouse Operations Management:


	Supervise all warehouse operations during assigned shifts.
	Ensure all inbound and outbound parcels are received, sorted, scanned, and dispatched accurately.
	Monitor warehouse workflow to achieve operational efficiency.
	Ensure all parcels are processed within approved timelines.
	Minimize parcel loss, damage, and misrouting.
	Ensure warehouse activities comply with Company policies and SOPs.


Staff Supervision & Leadership:


	Supervise Team Leads, Sorters, Warehouse Assistants, and other operational staff.
	Allocate daily responsibilities based on operational requirements.
	Conduct pre-shift and post-shift briefings.
	Monitor employee productivity and performance.
	Coach, mentor, and motivate team members to achieve operational targets.
	Enforce discipline and maintain a professional work environment.
	Recommend staff training and development where necessary.


Attendance & Manpower Management:


	Monitor staff attendance and punctuality.
	Ensure employees resume work on time and remain at their assigned workstations throughout their shift.
	Prepare daily attendance reports.
	Report absenteeism, lateness, misconduct, or unauthorized movement to the Human Resources Department.
	Ensure adequate manpower is available for all operational activities.
	Recommend additional manpower where operational demands require.


Parcel Sorting & Dispatch:


	Ensure parcels are sorted accurately according to destination, delivery route, and hub.
	Monitor scanning activities to ensure accurate system updates.
	Verify parcel quantities before loading and dispatch.
	Ensure priority shipments are processed promptly.
	Prevent dispatch errors and parcel misrouting.
	Monitor dispatch completion to meet delivery timelines.


Operational Performance Management:


	Monitor daily operational targets and Key Performance Indicators (KPIs).
	Ensure all customer delivery timelines, including priority client timelines (e.g., TEMU), are achieved.
	Identify operational bottlenecks and implement corrective actions.
	Reduce warehouse backlog through effective planning and supervision.
	Continuously improve warehouse productivity and efficiency.


Quality Control & Compliance:


	Ensure compliance with all Company Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs).
	Monitor parcel handling standards to reduce damage and losses.
	Ensure accurate documentation of all operational activities.
	Support Quality Control investigations relating to missing, damaged, or delayed parcels.
	Ensure compliance with operational audits and inspections.


Health, Safety & Security:


	Maintain a safe, clean, and organized warehouse environment.
	Ensure employees comply with health and safety regulations.
	Report workplace accidents, hazards, and security incidents immediately.
	Ensure Company property and operational equipment are safeguarded.
	Enforce security procedures relating to parcel handling and warehouse access.


Equipment & Asset Management:


	Monitor the proper use of warehouse equipment, scanners, laptops, printers, pallet trucks, and other operational tools.
	Report damaged equipment promptly.
	Ensure equipment is maintained and properly stored after use.
	Support periodic inventory and asset verification exercises.


Reporting:


	Prepare and submit accurate operational reports, including:
	Daily Shift Reports
	Attendance Reports
	Parcel Sorting Reports
	Dispatch Reports
	Warehouse Backlog Reports
	Productivity Reports
	Incident Reports
	Equipment Damage Reports
	Operational Improvement Reports


Customer Service Support:


	Work closely with the Customer Service and Quality Control teams to resolve customer complaints relating to warehouse operations.
	Investigate delayed shipments, misplaced parcels, and operational discrepancies.
	Provide timely feedback on operational issues affecting customer satisfaction.


Continuous Improvement:


	Identify opportunities to improve warehouse processes.
	Recommend productivity improvement initiatives.
	Participate in process improvement projects.
	Implement Management directives aimed at improving operational efficiency.
	Encourage teamwork and continuous learning within the operations team.
	Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)
	Parcel sorting accuracy.
	Dispatch completion within approved timelines.
	Warehouse productivity.
	Reduction in parcel loss and damage.
	Compliance with TEMU and other customer delivery timelines.
	Staff attendance and punctuality.
	Reduction in operational errors.
	Warehouse turnaround time.
	Employee productivity.
	Compliance with SOPs.
	Safety compliance.
	Customer complaint reduction.
	Warehouse cleanliness and organization.


Remuneration
N200,000 – N250,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected]g and copy: [email protected]g using the  Job Position as the subject of the mail.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Senior, ITTS Advisory",
  "description": "Ernst &amp; Young (EY) is a global leader in assurance, tax, transaction and advisory services. The insights and quality services we deliver help build trust and confidence in the capital markets and in economies the world over. We develop outstanding leaders who team to deliver on our promises to all of our stakeholders. In so doing, we play a critical role in building a better working world for our people, for our clients and for our communities.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Senior, ITTS Advisory

Requisition ID: 1696839&nbsp;
Job Location: Lagos
Job type: Full-time

Your tasks


	Advice on cross-border restructuring and financing concepts, international group tax law, foreign tax law, company acquisitions and sales

Senior, ITTS Advisory Job at Ernst & Young (EY)




        
    

        
        Ernst & Young (EY) is a global leader in assurance, tax, transaction and advisory services. The insights and quality services we deliver help build trust and confidence in the capital markets and in economies the world over. We develop outstanding leaders who team to deliver on our promises to all of our stakeholders. In so doing, we play a critical role in building a better working world for our people, for our clients and for our communities.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Senior, ITTS Advisory

Requisition ID: 1696839 
Job Location: Lagos
Job type: Full-time

Your tasks
     

            
    




	Advice on cross-border restructuring and financing concepts, international group tax law, foreign tax law, company acquisitions and sales
	Tax structuring of corporate transactions and reorganizations, identification of tax risks in the context of tax due diligence and conducting negotiations with sellers and Support of multinational companies in the planning, implementation and documentation of transfer pricing systems, the structuring of cross-border value creation processes as well as the support of tax audits, mutual agreement procedures and advance pricing agreements (APA)
	Management of complex tax projects, coordination of various audit and consulting assignments as well as motivation of your team members and promotion of their professional and personal development.


What you bring to the table
Skills that will help you shape the future


	Degree in Economics or Law or Degree in Finance, ideally passed tax  exam - CTIN
	At least five years of professional experience in international tax consulting, ideally with a specialization in transfer pricing or transaction projects
	Very good communication skills in English.


What sets you apart – qualities for your success:


	Analytical skills and precise way of working
	Empathetic personality with clear objectives and an entrepreneurial mindset.


Salary
Competitive.
